constraints_to_preserve: Karim: "DO THINGS IN THE ORDER I TELL YOU".
Karim: "i DEFIFNELY ALSO want the use last frame button iimnplemented as well as the below FEATURE LIST for OGAI".
Karim: "i am testing omi now and i would want to add that FIRST then do the other phases".
Karim wants the logo generated with image generation and used in the app; required source should be square 1:1, preferred 1024x1024 transparent PNG, readable at 32x32 in the current 56px header.
Karim wants add-to-prompt actions to append to existing prompt/input state with a space or line, not replace existing selections.
Karim wants generated videos added as prompt references only for the new Gemini Omni provider for now.
Karim wants output filename version counters to reset per prefix, but if reverting to an old prefix the app should continue from that prefix's last used version.
Karim wants delete warnings for uploads that are tagged in a Project or Reference, but no warning merely because an upload was used as a generation input.
Karim wants prompt templates as plain Markdown files for now.
Karim wants Uploads view to replace the generated images/videos in the center of the screen.
Karim wants References navigation as a cascading menu: References -> Characters/Locations/Props -> named collection -> open that page.

Future feature workflow in feature-dev/README.md must be followed: clarify requirements, refresh Graphify, trace code, trace local skills/scripts/libraries, read official docs, delegate research to subagents, plan in GPT-5.5 high or extra-high, audit via Multica with GLM 5.2 OpenCode and Gemini 3.5 Flash General, verify audit findings, then stop before execution until Karim confirms coding agents.

Default coding agents for execution are GPT-5.5 medium native executor subagents unless Karim chooses GLM 5.2 OpenCode or Composer 2.5 coding agents if available. GPT-5.5 high native code-reviewer agents should review before merge eligibility.

Do not break already-working native media paths while adding features. Verify Grok video, Nano Banana 2/Pro image, native Codex image, prompt copy, delete, history hydration, and existing .native-media assets before handing back for testing.
Do not serve fake provider results as successful user-facing generations.
Preserve NATIVE_MEDIA_ROOT and .native-media across branches/merges; do not clean or delete .native-media.
key_decisions_and_rationale: Feature-dev context now lives under /home/k8r1m/Open-Generative-AI/feature-dev so future planning artifacts stay with the fork instead of being scattered through old docs/plans files.
The current next-feature file is an inbox, not a plan, because Karim has not yet switched the session into plan mode or confirmed coding agents for execution.
Logo source recommendation is 1:1 1024x1024 PNG because the current app header uses a 32x32 square logo container inside a 56px header, so a square source downscales cleanly without changing layout.

References/projects should avoid duplicate media copies where possible, using metadata/tags/collection records pointing at the single stored asset; this matches Karim's stated preference but still needs exact schema planning.
Gemini Omni provider should be planned first because Karim explicitly prioritized it and it may affect the multimodal native media input contract.
issues_discovered: No new code issues were discovered in the docs-only feature inbox update.

During the earlier merge verification, one native scheduler cancellation test failed once in the full sweep, then passed in isolation and passed again in the full 232-test rerun. Treat as possible timing flake if it reappears.
Tailscale serve CLI required the corrected form tailscale serve --bg --tcp=19400 //127.0.0.1:19400 off to remove the stale 19400 TCP forward.
Gemini Omni official model/API name, exact supported modalities, and Vertex availability are not verified yet and must be researched before planning.

Whether every sidebar tab replaces generated history or only specific content pages such as Uploads and selected Reference/Project pages remains partially open.
